extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC Primoz attacked the ITT climb after mechanical delay, taking 40 seconds from THOMAS Geraint to overturn 26-second deficit and win Giro by 14 seconds
tactical_patterns: ROGLIC recovered 40s on THOMAS despite mid-climb mechanical, showing superior ITT climbing power erases equipment losses on steep gradients
THOMAS changed both bike and helmet at intermediate point, losing critical seconds in transition on 25-meter exchange zone
KUSS Sepp's +2s intermediate time foreshadowed teammate ROGLIC's dominance, revealing Jumbo-Visma pacing intel advantage

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: absorbed mechanical setback then gained 32s on final climb section, ITT climbing strength underestimated in stage race scenarios
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: faded badly on steepest gradients despite leading early, 40s loss reveals ceiling on ultra-steep ITT climbs
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: held third by 1m55s in mountain ITT, credible podium defense signals emerging Portuguese GC capability
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: positive: early wave reference time pressured GC rivals, stage winner versatility extends to mountain ITTs
